title: iberis carnosa subsp. lagascana
intro: Iberis carnosa subsp. lagascana, also known as Candytuft, is a flowering plant species in the Brassicaceae family. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Iberis carnosa subsp. lagascana have been linked to several health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and the treatment of skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
